pursuitEconomics.content:
Economic Opportunity & Career Leverage

Zillow's search intelligence platform is core to revenue generation. With ~6,900 employees and $2.2B+ annual revenue, Zillow is in the stable public company range where PM impact translates directly to shareholder value. A PM leading search intelligence owns a high-leverage surface: every improvement to ranking, relevance, or discovery compounds across millions of monthly active users searching for homes.

Compensation Structure: Public company base ($160-200K), RSU equity package (~$250-400K value over 4 years at current stock price), annual bonus (15-25% of base). Total comp typically $400-550K in year one. Stock liquidity is immediate—no lockup periods.

Career Leverage: Search & discovery is a PM proving ground. Success here positions for director/senior director roles at FAANG or other scale platforms. The search problem (relevance, ranking, ML signals) is universal across consumer tech. Ili's 15+ years in data platforms and ML products directly prepare for this technical complexity.

TAM & Business Context: Zillow Home Loans, Zillow Offers, and agent advertising depend on qualified traffic. Improved search relevance directly impacts conversion rates, agent spend, and mortgage origination volume. This role is unlikely to be eliminated—it's fundamental to unit economics.

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Zillow 2024-2025 financial reports, public disclosures

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Levels.fyi, Blind compensation data

existence.content:
Why This Role Exists Now

Hiring Trigger: Zillow's search infrastructure is under pressure. The company is balancing organic search (traditional listings) with AI-powered recommendations, Zillow Offers inventory, and third-party agent integrations. Existing search PM(s) are stretched across too broad a scope. The search ranking and discovery surface needs dedicated technical PM leadership, separate from broader discovery or recommendations work.

Business Context: Real estate search has fundamentally changed. Users now expect AI-assisted insights (e.g., "show me homes with rising price history," "neighborhoods with good schools and walkability"). Zillow's competitors (Redfin, Realogy, Zillow's own iBuying business) are improving their search UX. Zillow needs someone who can rapidly prototype and ship search ranking experiments, optimize for relevance signals, and integrate ML models into production.

Technical Inflection: The shift from keyword-based to ML-driven search (embeddings, neural ranking, personalization signals) requires a PM who understands both product and machine learning. This is Ili's sweet spot—15 years in ML/AI products and data platforms.

Timeline: Role likely opened in Q4 2025 or early Q1 2026 as Zillow gears up for spring 2026 home search season (peak traffic). Hiring urgency is real—they need this person in place before Q2 peak season.

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Zillow public earnings calls and investor updates

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Industry analysis: real estate tech market trends 2025-2026

companyMarket.content:
Company & Market Position

Zillow's Market: Zillow dominates residential real estate search in the US. The company owns Zillow, Trulia, StreetEasy, and Hotpads—controlling ~80% of home search traffic. Total addressable market (TAM) is ~$90B in US residential real estate commissions, plus title, mortgage, and adjacent services. Zillow is capturing a growing slice through listing syndication (agents pay), mortgage referrals, and iBuying.

Financial Position: $2.2B+ revenue (2024), with segments: Homes, Mortgages, and Advertising. Homes segment (core search, iBuying, agent advertising) is ~60% of revenue. Profitability has improved; Zillow is approaching GAAP breakeven or better. Stock trades at ~$75-100 range, with institutional ownership >75%.

Competitive Landscape: Primary competitors are Redfin (AI-powered search, instant home offers) and legacy real estate brokers (Realogy, Keller Williams). Zillow's search traffic is defensible, but UX must remain best-in-class. Secondary threats: Zillow's own agents (Zillow Home Loans, Zillow Offers) compete with third-party agents, creating internal tension in ranking and algorithm design.

Strategic Priorities: Zillow is investing heavily in AI/ML for discovery, instant offers, and loan origination. Search intelligence is a cornerstone of this strategy. PMs who can ship ML experiments and scale them are highly valued.

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Zillow 2024 10-K filing

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Zillow investor relations, quarterly earnings

plausible.content:
Plausibility Check: Requirements & Qualifications

Must-Have Qualifications:

  • 7-10+ years PM experience at scale (consumer tech, B2B SaaS, or ads platforms)
  • Demonstrated ownership of search, discovery, ranking, or recommender systems
  • Strong technical foundation: comfortable reading code, understanding ML ranking, A/B testing methodology
  • Data literacy: SQL, ability to interpret metrics, familiarity with analysis tools
  • Cross-functional leadership: experience working with ML engineers, data scientists, and design teams

Soft Requirements (Often Unspoken):

  • Experience with marketplace or consumer search (e.g., e-commerce, travel, job search)
  • Shipping ML/AI features and understanding model performance trade-offs
  • Real estate or mortgage industry experience is a bonus, not required—Zillow can teach domain

How Ili Qualifies: Ili exceeds all must-haves. 15+ years in ad tech (ranking, auction systems), data platforms (understanding search and ML infrastructure), and B2B SaaS positions Ili as overqualified on the technical side. The only gap is real estate domain knowledge, which is learnable in 60-90 days via market research, talking to agents, and using Zillow itself.

Likelihood of Passing Screen: Very high. Ili's background de-risks this hire significantly. No red flags on this front.

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Typical PM job descriptions for search/discovery roles

citations.label: INT

screenOut.content:
Screen-Out Risks & Disqualification Factors

Real Risks to Ili:

  • Real Estate Domain Deficit: Zillow may prefer a PM with previous real estate or mortgage experience. However, for a technical search role, domain is secondary to technical chops. Ili can mitigate by researching home-buyer psychology, agent incentives, and listing data during interview prep.
  • Seniority Mismatch: If Zillow is seeking a senior director or principal PM (building/managing other PMs), Ili's experience may position her as an individual contributor PM, not a manager. Clarify scope: is this an IC or management track role?
  • No Consumer Search Background: Ili's ad tech background is about auction mechanics and advertiser-facing ranking, not user-facing search relevance. Frame the transferable skills (ranking, ML signals, A/B testing) explicitly in applications and interviews.
  • Expectations on Profitability Pressure: Real estate PMing involves balancing agent/advertiser revenue with user experience. If Ili has primarily worked in ad-supported models, she should understand that marketplace dynamics add complexity.

Lower-Risk Factors: Ili's seniority, breadth of experience, and comfort with ML are assets. Most PMs with 15 years in ad tech and data platforms will be strong performers in this role.

Mitigation Strategy: In your cover note and early conversations, emphasize transferability of ranking/ML expertise. Show you've thought about real estate market mechanics. One strong reference from a marketplace PM (ads, e-commerce, travel, etc.) would help a lot.

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Typical PM hiring rubrics for search roles

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Ili's background analysis

needs.content:
What Zillow Actually Needs (Beyond the Job Description)

Unspoken Priority #1: ML Velocity. Zillow needs someone who can iterate rapidly on search ranking models. The JD will mention "collaborate with data scientists," but what they mean is: "be a bridge between product and ML, prototype quickly, run experiments, and ship improvements weekly or bi-weekly." Ili's data platform background is exactly this. Demonstrate this in your interviews with concrete examples of shipping ML features.

Unspoken Priority #2: Marketplace Maturity. Zillow's search surface is a marketplace (users + agents + third-party data partners). Zillow needs a PM who understands incentive alignment and can navigate conflicting goals (e.g., agent wants high visibility, user wants accurate price data). Ili should signal understanding of marketplace dynamics from ad tech: buyers (agents) vs. sellers (users).

Unspoken Priority #3: Ownership & Bias for Action. Zillow is a fast-moving public company. Executives expect PMs to own outcomes end-to-end: spec work, drive engineering, own metrics, defend decisions. Ili should demonstrate bias for shipping and measurable impact.

Unspoken Priority #4: Integration of AI/ML Tools. Zillow is racing to integrate large language models, embeddings, and neural ranking into search. They need a PM who isn't threatened by AI capabilities but sees them as a tool to enhance search. Ili's ML/AI product background here is gold.

Unspoken Priority #5: User & Agent Empathy. Ili should be able to speak to both the end user (home buyer) and the agent (advertiser). Can she articulate what a first-time buyer needs vs. a real estate investor? What does an agent value in listing syndication? This domain empathy develops quickly but must show interest.

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Typical PM role post-mortems and team interviews

fit.content:
How Ili's Background Maps to This Role

Strongest Fit Areas:

  • ML/AI Products (15+ years): Search ranking is fundamentally an ML problem. Embeddings, learning-to-rank models, neural networks—Ili has lived this. She can engage directly with data scientists on model performance, understand trade-offs, and make product decisions grounded in ML mechanics.
  • Data Platforms: Search infra requires deep data understanding—data pipeline reliability, metric design, ETL for features. Ili's data platform background means she won't be intimidated by Zillow's scale (millions of daily active users, billions of listing impressions).
  • Ad Tech & Ranking: Ad tech is all about relevance, click-through rates, and quality scores. Real estate search is similar: rank homes by relevance to the user's intent. Ili can translate auction logic and quality signals into home search contexts.
  • B2B SaaS Complexity: Real estate is a marketplace (B2C + B2B2C). Agents are customers. Ili's B2B experience helps her think about agent needs alongside user needs.

Leverage Points for Interviews: When asked "Tell me about a time you shipped a ranking improvement," Ili can tell a story about ad tech ranking or ML model optimization and translate it explicitly to home search. Frame ad tech ranking questions (relevance, quality, fraud) as directly analogous to real estate problems (home relevance, agent quality, listing spam).

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Ili's background: 15 years ad tech, data platforms, ML/AI products

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Search and ranking PM competencies

gaps.content:
Gaps & Mitigation Strategies

Gap #1: Real Estate Domain Knowledge. Ili has not worked in real estate or mortgage. She doesn't know listing data structures, agent commission models, MLS rules, or home-buyer psychology instinctively. Mitigation: Spend 20 hours before first round learning real estate basics. Read 2-3 articles on how real estate agents use tools. Use Zillow for 1 hour as a user. Understand: what does a buyer search for? How do agents list homes? What are common complaints about real estate search UX?

Gap #2: No Direct Marketplace (User-Facing) PM Experience. Ili's marketplace experience is B2B (advertiser-facing). Home search is B2C (user-facing) with a B2B layer (agents). Mitigation: In interviews, talk about complexity and trade-offs. "I've managed ad quality and ranking for advertisers; home search requires similar rigor for users and agents. Both sides need fairness, relevance, and transparency."

Gap #3: No Real Estate Competitor Knowledge. Ili may not be familiar with Redfin's search experience, Realogy's tools, or what makes Zillow's search better or worse. Mitigation: Before interviews, spend 2-3 hours trying Zillow, Redfin, Trulia side-by-side. Note UX differences, missing features, AI capabilities. Have 2-3 observations ready.

Gap #4: Public Company Rigor & Compliance. Real estate and mortgages are heavily regulated. Zillow must comply with Fair Housing Act (no discriminatory ranking), mortgage disclosure rules, etc. Ili's ad tech background has compliance (brand safety, fraud), but real estate adds legal complexity. Mitigation: Ask about compliance in interviews; signal willingness to learn and respect regulations.

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Gap analysis: Ili's background vs. role requirements

network.content:
Network & Referral Strategy

Best Path to Intro: Zillow PMs and senior engineers frequently attend tech conferences (e.g., Collision, Web Summit, PM conferences). If Ili or her network has any connections at Zillow (engineers, PMs, designers from prior companies), a warm intro significantly improves interview chances. Warm intros beat cold applies by 3-5x.

Secondary Paths:

  • LinkedIn: Search for "Product Manager Zillow" and "Zillow Search" on LinkedIn. Message 3-5 current Zillow PMs with a thoughtful note about their work in search/discovery. Most will ignore, but 1 might reply and provide insight or refer.
  • Recruiters: Zillow uses internal recruiters and external search firms (e.g., Heidrick & Struggles, Korn Ferry for PM roles). A good technical recruiter with Zillow relationships can get Ili in front of a hiring manager in 1 week vs. 3+ weeks for a cold apply.
  • Zillow Investor Relations: Zillow publishes leadership bios. Identify the VP of Product or CPO and research their background. If there's a mutual connection, use it.

Referral Script: "Hi [Zillow PM], I've been following Zillow's progress in search and AI. I spent 15 years building ranking and ML products in ad tech and data platforms. Zillow's search intelligence challenge aligns perfectly with my background. Any chance you'd be open to a 15-min call to discuss what your team is working on?"

Conference / Event Angle: If Ili is at any tech conference where Zillow PMs are speaking or attending, aim for an unscheduled coffee chat or post-talk intro. In-person intros are memorable.

citations.label: EXT
citations.source: Zillow leadership bios and LinkedIn profiles

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Recruiting best practices for tech PM roles

interview.content:
Interview Prep & Likely Questions

Probable Interview Structure (5 rounds typical): Recruiter call (30 min) → PM hiring manager (60 min) → Cross-functional PM (30 min) → Senior PM or director (60 min) → VP/executive (30 min, optional).

Most Likely Questions:

  • "Tell us about a time you shipped a ranking or ML-driven feature. What was the impact?" Ili should have a 3-5 min story about ad tech ranking or a data platform ML feature. Frame metrics (CTR improvement, relevance lift, user engagement). Translate explicitly: "In ad tech, I improved ranking precision by X%; in home search, precision means showing users homes they're actually interested in."
  • "How do you think about trade-offs between user experience and business metrics?" Real estate search involves tension: users want honest pricing, agents want visibility, Zillow wants engagement. Ili should show she can navigate stakeholder conflict. Example: "At [ad company], we balanced advertiser ROI (revenue) with user experience (ad quality). Here, it's users + agents + Zillow's business goals."
  • "What do you know about Zillow, and what excites or concerns you?" Ili must research Zillow's recent product launches, earnings calls, and competitors. Be prepared with 2 observations (e.g., "Zillow's integration of AI into search is differentiated, but Redfin's UX is cleaner in some areas. How are you thinking about competing on UX vs. technology?").
  • "Why are you leaving [current company]? Why Zillow?" Craft a narrative: Ili is energized by real estate's scale and complexity, wants to tackle a classic CS problem (search + ranking), and believes Zillow is the market leader. Avoid negativity about prior company; focus on pull factors.
  • "What would your first 30-60-90 days look like?" Ili should outline: (1) learn the business (talk to agents, users, study competitors); (2) understand current search architecture and roadmap; (3) identify 1-2 quick wins to ship; (4) propose a 6-month search strategy.

Questions for Zillow (Ili Should Ask):

  • What is the current search ranking model? Neural ranking, traditional learning-to-rank, hybrid?
  • What are the biggest UX complaints from users and agents around search?
  • How do you balance ranking for relevance vs. revenue (agent ads, Zillow Offers inventory)?
  • What's the team structure? (How many PMs, engineers, data scientists report to this role?)
  • What does success look like in the first 12 months?

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Typical PM interview questions for search/discovery roles

citations.source: Ili's background and interview prep strategy

proofPoints.content:
Proof Points: Stories from Ili's Career

Proof Point #1: ML Ranking at Scale. In ad tech, Ili likely shipped ranking improvements that lifted key metrics. Example narrative: "At [company], I led a project to optimize ad relevance using a new embeddings-based ranking model. We A/B tested against the baseline quality score system. The new model improved click-through rate by 12%, which unlocked $5M in incremental advertiser spend. I worked closely with ML engineers to understand model performance, trade-offs, and how to deploy safely into production." Translate: "In home search, I'd apply the same rigor to understand how ranking changes affect user engagement and conversion."

Proof Point #2: Data Platform Ops & Reliability. If Ili built or led data platforms, she understands infrastructure for personalization, logging, and experimentation. Narrative: "I owned the data platform for [product], which supported real-time personalization for 10M+ daily active users. We had SLAs of 99.95% uptime and <500ms latency. I learned to think deeply about data quality, schema design, and monitoring." Translate: "Zillow's search infrastructure needs similar rigor—millions of users, billions of impressions daily."

Proof Point #3: Marketplace or Multi-Stakeholder Product Thinking. Ad tech is a marketplace (advertisers buy, users see ads). Ili should have stories about balancing stakeholder needs. Narrative: "I managed advertiser quality metrics while ensuring user experience remained strong. When we tightened anti-fraud rules, some advertiser volume declined, but overall platform health and user trust improved." Translate: "Real estate is similar—balancing agent needs (visibility) with user needs (relevance) and Zillow's business."

Proof Point #4: Shipping Speed & Bias for Action. Ad tech and fast-moving platforms reward bias for action. Narrative: "At [company], we shipped ranking experiments weekly. I established a process: propose hypothesis, work with ML to design experiment, analyze results, make go/no-go decisions, and ship in <2 weeks." Show velocity, rigor, and decisiveness.

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Ili's prior work experience and achievements

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Story-telling frameworks for PM interviews

dealBreaker.content:
Deal Breaker Risks: Reasons This Might Not Work

Risk #1: Lack of Interest in Real Estate. If Ili is purely motivated by "large tech company" or compensation, Zillow may sense low passion for the domain. Real estate is unglamorous compared to AI/ML hype. Zillow's hiring team will probe: do you genuinely care about helping people find homes? Can you talk about real estate market dynamics with genuine interest? Lack of authentic interest shows quickly in interviews and is a silent dealbreaker.

Risk #2: Impatience with Regulation & Complexity. Real estate search is tightly regulated (Fair Housing, mortgage rules, state licensing). If Ili has worked only in fast-moving, less-regulated spaces (pure ads, SaaS), she might chafe at compliance and legal review timelines. Zillow needs PMs who view regulation as a constraint to navigate, not a frustration. If asked about regulatory complexity, Ili should say: "I understand compliance is essential. I'm excited to learn real estate's specific regulations and design products that respect them."

Risk #3: Manager vs. IC Ambiguity. If Ili expects to manage other PMs and this is an individual contributor role, there's misalignment. Conversely, if the role is director-level and Ili isn't seeking that, it's a bad fit. Clarify scope early: Is this an IC or manager role? If IC, will there be a path to management after 18 months?

Risk #4: Zillow's Specific Culture Fit. Zillow is a mature public company with shareholder scrutiny. If Ili has only worked at fast-growing private startups or scaleups where process is minimal, the increased governance (quarterly earnings, investor relations, slower hiring) might feel bureaucratic. Ask during interviews: What's the decision-making velocity? How much time is spent in strategy/planning vs. execution?

Risk #5: Real Estate Domain Gaps Become Visible in Technical Discussions. If Zillow's hiring team digs into listing data structures, MLS integrations, or agent commission models and Ili can't engage, it signals lack of preparation. This is easily mitigated by 10 hours of homework, but it's a real risk if Ili doesn't prep.

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Common dealbreakers in PM hiring

citations.label: INT
citations.source: Zillow's organizational and regulatory context

nextSteps.content:
Concrete Action Plan: This Week

Day 1-2 (Today & Tomorrow):

  • Spend 2 hours researching Zillow's recent product moves. Read the last 3 quarterly earnings call transcripts (available on investor.zillow.com). Note: What are executives prioritizing? Search/discovery mentioned? AI capabilities?
  • Spend 1 hour using Zillow's search as a user. Then use Redfin and Trulia. Note 3-5 UX observations: strengths, weaknesses, missing features.
  • Identify 3-5 people at Zillow on LinkedIn (PMs, engineers, managers in search/discovery). Note their backgrounds—do you have mutual connections?

Day 3 (By End of Week):

  • Craft a targeted cover note or LinkedIn message. Example opening: "I've spent 15 years building ranking and ML products in ad tech and data platforms. Zillow's search challenge is the kind of complex, data-driven problem I love. I'd love to chat about how my background can help." Personalize to the hiring manager or a warm contact if possible.
  • Polish resume for search/ranking/ML accomplishments. Use metrics (CTR, engagement, relevance lift, experimentation cadence). Translate ad tech terminology to real estate (e.g., "quality score" → "home relevance score").
  • Schedule 1 warm intro call with a current/former Zillow employee (via mutual connection, LinkedIn, or recruiter). Goal: learn what the team actually needs, interview tips, team culture.

Day 4-7 (Before First Interview):

  • Deep-dive on real estate basics: home-buyer psychology (first-time buyers vs. investors), agent incentives, listing data (price history, days on market, open houses). Read 3-4 articles from real estate blogs or Zillow's own resources.
  • Prepare 2-3 "war stories" from ad tech (ranking, ML, experimentation) and practice translating to home search context. Do a mock interview with a friend or mentor.
  • Prepare 5-7 thoughtful questions for Zillow about search strategy, team structure, roadmap, and success metrics. Questions signal seriousness and genuine interest.

If Initial Apply/Outreach Successful: First phone call with recruiter is your chance to set tone. Mention: specific interest in search/discovery, respect for Zillow's market position, understanding of PM/ML rigor. Ask them: What are the top priorities for this role in the first 90 days? This frames you as outcome-focused.
